vignette: 25-year-old female, 2 days of runny nose, mild cough, low-grade fever (99.2F), sore throat. No travel, no contacts, exam unremarkable.

data.vignette: 25-year-old female, 2 days of runny nose, mild cough, low-grade fever (99.2F), sore throat. No travel, no contacts, exam unremarkable.

data.reasoning: The patient presents with classic symptoms of an upper respiratory tract infection (URTI): runny nose, mild cough, low-grade fever, and sore throat. The presentation is straightforward, with no red flags such as hemodynamic instability, neurological deficits, suspected malignancy, severe infection, multi-system involvement, atypical or rapidly progressive symptoms, or immunocompromise. The physical exam is also unremarkable. This presentation is common and confidently diagnosable by a general physician.
